title: Police investigating after dead body found near home owned by Pressley’s husband
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police found a dead body near a rental property in Mattapan owned by Ayanna Pressley's husband.
Officers responded to 25 Malta St. for an 'investigate person' call and discovered an unresponsive body.
The Mattapan property, valued at over $919,000, is on the market and was raided two months ago.

title: Healey aims to close great divide of resale ticket prices with new proposal
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Governor Maura Healey proposes 'The Great Divide Act' to cap Massachusetts resale ticket prices at 110% of original value.
The act aims to prohibit deceptive ticketing practices and limit reseller service fees to 10% of the original ticket price.
Vermont singer-songwriter Noah Kahan supports the legislation, following similar efforts to regulate ticket prices in Vermont.

title: Rhode Island couple unexpectedly become parents while on vacation in Florida
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lauren Siwinski and Gary Glenn unexpectedly became parents while on vacation in Florida before a planned cruise.
Doctors at HCA Florida Brandon Hospital informed Siwinski she was in labor, surprising the couple as they were unaware of the pregnancy.
Their son, Sebastian, was born shortly thereafter, prompting a visit from his grandmother to meet the newborn in Florida.

title: Juvenile humpback whale washes ashore in Winthrop
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 35,000-pound juvenile humpback whale was found dead under a private dock in Winthrop, prompting an investigation.
The whale's cause of death remains unknown, and a necropsy is planned with assistance from NOAA officials.
This is the first large whale stranding in Massachusetts in 2026, with six reported in the state in 2024.

title: Jahmai Jones hits memorable first home run for Red Sox
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jahmai Jones hit a two-run homer in his second at-bat for the Red Sox against Tampa Bay Rays.
Jones's home run tied the game 2-2 at Fenway Park, marking his first for the Red Sox.
Jones has struggled in 2026 with a .135 average before his trade from the Tigers to Boston.

title: Wilyer Abreu joins elite Red Sox company following his back-to-back multi-HR games
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Wilyer Abreu became the first Red Sox player since 2016 to have back-to-back multi-home run games.
Abreu hit four home runs in two games against the Tampa Bay Rays at Fenway Park on July 17-18, 2026.
He is only the second Red Sox player ever, after George Scott, to achieve this at Fenway Park.

title: Red Sox extend season-altering win streak to 12 games in epic comeback fashion
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox extend their win streak to 12 games with a comeback victory over the Tampa Bay Rays.
Wilyer Abreu's multi-home run game was crucial in overcoming a three-run deficit for the Red Sox win.
Red Sox hold the American League's final wild-card spot, with contenders Seattle and Baltimore close behind.

title: Report: LeBron James ‘hates’ Celtics, would rank them last on his list of teams to join
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James likely to avoid joining the Boston Celtics due to a longstanding rivalry.
Journalist Brian Windhorst reports Boston Celtics rank last on LeBron's preferred teams list.
James's decision is reportedly delaying the NBA 2026-27 schedule release amid free agency speculation.

title: Tom Brady busy at Fanatics Fest, from a face slap to showing off his arm
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tom Brady slapped Logan Paul at Fanatics Fest in New York City, sparking viral interest.
Brady and Paul had a public feud over athleticism, intensified by a flag football match in March.
Brady also demonstrated his athletic skills, throwing a baseball and kicking a football at the event.

title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men charged with kidnapping and assault after allegedly attacking a 28-year-old near Winter Park.
Police say the victim was bound with an extension cord and suffered facial injuries during the alleged attack.
Suspects identified as Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett; all released on bail pending arraignment.
